title: 3D Force-Directed Network Graph (Social Network Visualization)
prompt:
Use Three.js to implement a 3D force-directed graph for social network visualization.

[Three.js Import (must follow exactly)]
The HTML <head> must include the following importmap (verbatim), and import via ES Module in <script type="module">:
<script type="importmap">
{ "imports": {
    "three": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.170.0/build/three.module.js",
    "three/addons/": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.170.0/examples/jsm/"
} }
</script>
<script type="module">
import * as THREE from 'three';
import { OrbitControls } from 'three/addons/controls/OrbitControls.js';
// Your code here
</script>

[Graph Data]
(1) Hardcode a social network in the code: exactly 50 nodes and 80 undirected edges.
(2) Each node contains: name (string), group (integer 0-4, 5 groups total). Each edge contains source and target (node index or id).

[Force-Directed Simulation]
(3) Implement a per-frame force-directed simulation yourself (do not use a black-box graph library that wraps the entire simulation, but you may reference classic formulas):
    - Coulomb repulsion between nodes (all-pairs repulsion)
    - Spring attraction along edges (attraction along edges, natural length can be the geometric distance at creation or a fixed restLength)
    - Weak centering force toward the origin to prevent global drift
(4) Use semi-implicit Euler or similar numerical integration with a reasonably small dt; pinned nodes have their position and velocity fixed at 0 (they do not participate in movement).

[Rendering]
(5) Nodes are spheres (THREE.Mesh), with 5 distinct colors for 5 groups.
(6) Node radius (or scale) monotonically related to degree (more connections = larger).
(7) Edges use THREE.Line or LineSegments, with vertex positions updated each frame as nodes move.
(8) OrbitControls for scene navigation.

[Interaction]
(9) Mouse hover on node: highlight that node and its neighbors (visually distinct), and display the node name in id="hoverInfo".
(10) Click on node: toggle that node's pin/unpin state; pinned nodes do not move during simulation.
(11) id="pauseBtn": button to toggle between pause and resume; when paused, stop integration updates but preserve the current layout.

[HUD]
(12) id="nodeCount": number of nodes (should be 50).
(13) id="edgeCount": number of edges (should be 80).
(14) id="simEnergy": display the simulation "energy/temperature" scalar (e.g., total kinetic energy plus a simplified potential energy, or sum of squared velocities; must be a finite number that varies during runtime).
(15) id="pinnedCount": current number of pinned nodes.

[State Exposure (required)]
Update window.__3D_STATE__ every frame, including at least:
- nodeCount: number
- edgeCount: number
- nodes: Array<{ x, y, z, name, group, pinned, degree }>
- simEnergy: number
- isPaused: boolean
- pinnedCount: number

Output a single HTML file that can be opened directly in a browser. All coordinates and energy values must be finite numbers; NaN/Infinity is not allowed.
physics_constraints: Coulomb repulsion + spring attraction along edges + centering force
Pinned nodes: zero velocity, fixed position during simulation steps
simEnergy finite whenever simulation state is queried
